items.headline: Wo Tanken besonders teuer ist - und wo sich sparen lässt
items.summary: Wer mit dem Auto unterwegs ist, kann je nach Land und Region unterschiedlich viel fürs Tanken bezahlen. Wo Sprit in Europa besonders teuer oder günstig ist und worauf Reisende achten sollten. Von Michelle Goddemeier.

items.headline: Tote bei Sturzfluten in afghanischer Provinz Nuristan
items.summary: Immer wieder gibt es verheerende Sturzfluten in Afghanistan. Nun starben in der Provinz Nuristan mindestens 23 Menschen. Viele werden noch in den Trümmern eingestürzter Gebäude vermisst.

items.headline: Heizungsaustausch: Wer jetzt mit wie viel Geld rechnen kann
items.summary: Für den Einbau von Wärmepumpen und klimafreundlichen Heizungen gelten neue Förderregeln. Welche Haushalte mit wie viel staatlicher Unterstützung rechnen können und was beim Antrag zu beachten ist. Von Ursula Mayer.

items.headline: Suche nach Unionsfraktionsspitze: Einer wäre Frei
items.summary: Bis nächste Woche die Unionsfraktion eine neue Spitze wählt, wird viel spekuliert. Ein Name, der immer wieder fällt: Thorsten Frei, derzeit Kanzleramtsminister. Für CDU-Chef und Kanzler Merz eröffnet der Spahn-Rücktritt neue Spielräume.

items.headline: Europas Banken im Fokus
items.summary: Europas Banken dürften nach den guten US-Vorgaben mit viel Rückenwind in die neue Berichtssaison gehen. Es werden weiter steigende Gewinne erwartet. Ein Unsicherheitsfaktor aber bleibt.

items.headline: Deutsche Bahn plant bundesweites Alkoholverbot an Bahnhöfen
items.summary: Die Deutsche Bahn will künftig an allen Bahnhöfen ein Alkoholverbot durchsetzen. Sie verspricht sich mehr Sicherheit für Mitarbeiter und Reisende. In Hamburg etwa hat man damit bereits gute Erfahrungen gemacht.

items.headline: UN-Report: Kleine Fortschritte, doch der Hunger bleibt
items.summary: Die Zahl der Hungernden sinkt, doch Konflikte wie im Nahen Osten könnten die Lebensmittelversorgung massiv verschlechtern. Milliarden Menschen fehlt zudem das Geld für eine gesunde Ernährung. Von C. Auerbach.

items.headline: Libanesischer Präsident Aoun trifft Trump in Washington
items.summary: Gegenüber der Hisbollah zeigt der libanesische Präsident Aoun klare Kante. Das kommt in Washington gut an. Nun hofft Aoun, den US-Präsidenten davon überzeugen zu können, mehr Druck auf Israel auszuüben. Von Carsten Kühntopp.

items.headline: Nextcloud: Wirrwarr um Website-Störung – Cyberangriff bestätigt
items.summary: Am Sonntag ging die Webseite von Nextcloud vorübergehend offline. Der Anbieter bestätigt auf Anfrage einen Cyberangriff.

items.headline: Suno-Datenleck: Have I Been Pwned ergänzt 55 Millionen Konten
items.summary: Das Have-I-Been-Pwned-Projekt hat mehr als 55 Millionen Konten aus dem Suno-Datenleck zur Datenhalde hinzugefügt.

items.headline: AliExpress: EU-Kommission verhängt DSA-Rekordstrafe
items.summary: Der chinesische Onlinemarktplatz AliExpress hat über Jahre gegen den Digital Services Act verstoßen und muss nun 550 Millionen Euro zahlen.

items.headline: Microsoft arbeitet an Lösung für WSUS-Störungen
items.summary: Die Update-Software WSUS hat seit vergangener Woche mit Problemen zu kämpfen, hat Microsoft nun eingeräumt. Es gibt erste Fixes.

items.headline: WTF: Billionenbeträge berechnet: Abrechnungsfehler bei Amazon Web Services
items.summary: Kunden wurden plötzlich astronomische Kosten für Amazon Web Services angezeigt. Dahinter steckt ein technischer Fehler.

items.headline: Moonshot AI präsentiert Kimi K3: Chinas Antwort auf OpenAI und Anthropic
items.summary: Chinas KI-Start-up Moonshot AI setzt die USA mit seinem neuen Sprachmodell unter Druck. Wie reagieren Washington und das Silicon Valley auf den Wettbewerb?

items.headline: LHS 1140 b: Erstmals Atmosphäre bei erdähnlichem Exoplaneten nachgewiesen
items.summary: Seit seiner Entdeckung gilt LHS 1140 b als besonders interessant für die Suche nach außerirdischem Leben. Nun wurde bei dem Exoplaneten Helium entdeckt.

items.headline: Google Chrome: Außerplanmäßiges zweites Update in der Woche
items.summary: Google aktualisiert Chrome eigentlich jeden Mittwoch. Diese Woche folgt ein zweites Update, das mehrere kritische Lücken schließt.

items.headline: Splatoon Raiders im Test: Kunterbunter Solo-Volltreffer
items.summary: Erkundung und Monsterhorden: Wir haben Splatoon Raiders solo gespielt - und wurden spät, aber nachhaltig überzeugt (nur für Nintendo Switch 2). Ein Test von Peter Steinlechner (Splatoon, Spieletest)

items.headline: Anzeige: Kindle Scribe E-Reader mit Stift fällt um 130 Euro auf Tiefstpreis
items.summary: Wer den Alltag ohne Zettelwirtschaft strukturieren möchte, findet im Kindle Scribe den perfekten Begleiter. Amazon reduziert das 32-GB-Modell um 130 Euro. (Technik/Hardware, Kindle)

items.headline: Anzeige: Ladegerät mit acht USB-Ports für unter 55 Euro bei Amazon
items.summary: Amazon hat ein Ladegerät von Ugreen im Angebot, das mit einer Gesamtleistung von bis zu 200 W acht Geräte gleichzeitig schnell auflädt. (Technik/Hardware)

items.headline: Anzeige: Roborock-Saugroboter mit Absaugstation fällt auf Jahrestiefstpreis
items.summary: Die tägliche Bodenreinigung kostet Zeit. Amazon reduziert aktuell den Roborock-Q7-L5+-Saugroboter im Set auf einen neuen Jahrestiefstpreis unter 200 Euro. (Technik/Hardware, Saugroboter)

items.headline: Cirqa: Garmin bringt Whoop-Konkurrenten mit Taste
items.summary: Garmin hat Cirqa offiziell angekündigt. Der Fitnesstracker kommt ohne Display aus, hat eine einzelne Taste - aber vorerst kein Abo. (Sportuhr, Fitness)

items.headline: Astronomie: Wie alt ist das Universum?
items.summary: Dank neuer Daten zu alten Sternen hat ein Forschungsteam die Frage beantwortet, ob das Universum jünger ist als gedacht. Die Forschungsleiterin erklärt, wie. Ein Bericht von Dirk Eidemüller (Astronomie, James-Webb-Teleskop)

items.headline: Anzeige: LG QNED TV mit 75 Zoll ist bei Amazon jetzt 250 Euro günstiger
items.summary: Die besten Filme und Serien wollen in besonderer Bildqualität genossen werden. Bei Amazon ist ein LG QNED TV mit 75 Zoll jetzt stark reduziert. (LG, Heimkino)

items.headline: (g+) Miasma-Lieferkette: Warum gültige Signaturen plötzlich nicht mehr reichen
items.summary: Ein selbst verbreitender Wurm kapert npm-Pakete mit gültigen Signaturen. Worauf Dev-Teams jetzt achten sollten. Ein Ratgebertext von Steffen Zahn (NPM, Virus)

items.headline: Dozens hurt in Bologna protests after man dies while being restrained by police
items.summary: Thousands of people took to the streets demanding justice for a Moroccan-born man who died on Sunday.

items.headline: Row escalates in Ukraine over army chief's future
items.summary: Protesters are demanding Oleksandr Syrskyi be removed and former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov reinstated by Friday.

items.headline: Shock and anger after top Ghanaian politician sentenced to 20 years for illegal mining
items.summary: Bernard Antwi Boasiako, known as "Chairman Wontumi", was also fined more than $10,000 (£7,450).

items.headline: Trump slaps 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney vows to 'intensify' trade talks
items.summary: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

items.headline: Traffic officers arrested for allegedly seeking bribe from Nigeria's anti-corruption boss
items.summary: They are accused of stopping Musa Aliyu at a fake roadblock and telling him to drive to a cash machine.

items.headline: Watch: Flooded towns and evacuations as Chile hit by heavy rain
items.summary: A "state of catastrophe" has been declared in Coquimbo and Huasco following the downpour.

items.headline: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
items.summary: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

items.headline: Why are thousands protesting for education reform in India?
items.summary: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.

items.headline: AI minister role boosted but tech department axed in Burnham shake-up
items.summary: Kanishka Narayan will attend cabinet - but no longer have the leadership of a technology secretary.

items.headline: Thousands of websites taken down for illegal World Cup streams
items.summary: More than 1,000 illegal streaming sites were shut down in the US during the tournament, with a similar amount blocked in Colombia.

items.headline: Ofcom unable to take further legal action against suicide forum
items.summary: The regulator said it had "no further legal routes available" under the Online Safety Act.

items.headline: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for allowing sale of illegal goods
items.summary: The Chinese retailer allowed unsafe toys and counterfeit clothes to be sold on its site, says the EU.

items.headline: Tech Now
items.summary: Ammie Sekhon goes behind the scenes of WWE to see the tech that supports the show.

items.headline: China's Moonshot AI claims Kimi K3 can rival OpenAI and Anthropic
items.summary: The company has unveiled a massive new artificial intelligence model it says can take on top American firms.

items.headline: Trump Media to sell instant access to 'market-moving' social posts
items.summary: Trump Media is launching a fast, paid feed to its most influential posts for Wall Street traders.

items.headline: TikTok faces Ofcom investigation over child age checks
items.summary: It follows a review by the regulator in May that criticised the platform for not being "safe enough" for children.

items.headline: DA: Cop covered bodycam to snap nude prisoners on his iPhone—but other cams caught him
items.summary: Pennsylvania cop charged with oppression and obstruction.

items.headline: Firefighting drones in the works as wildfires plague US nearly year-round
items.summary: California and XPRIZE competition tests whether drones can stop wildfires early.

items.headline: Judge halts Paramount's $111B purchase of Warner Bros. in win for US states
items.summary: Judge grants restraining order, saying merger "likely to violate antitrust laws."

items.headline: The Space Force is now seeking to buy up to $30 billion in rocket launches
items.summary: The Trump administration is asking the Space Force to do a lot. This will require more launches.

items.headline: AT&T loses key ruling in bid to stop offering basic phone service in California
items.summary: AT&T suffers setback but will keep asking court and FCC to preempt state rules.

items.headline: RIP bargain bin: The price impact of Sony's disc-free PlayStation plan
items.summary: Used discs are often cheaper than even deep digital discounts.

items.headline: F1 in Belgium: Machine learning algorithms are ruining the sport
items.summary: Many will say Spa is the best racetrack on earth, but not for these F1 cars.

items.headline: AliExpress hit with record $625M fine after failing to make EU-ordered fixes
items.summary: Online retailer AliExpress says it's shocked by largest DSA fine yet.

items.headline: Gritt exits stealth with $34 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
items.summary: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.

items.headline: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
items.summary: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

items.headline: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
items.summary: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

items.headline: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
items.summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

items.headline: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
items.summary: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.

items.headline: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
items.summary: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

items.headline: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
items.summary: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business.

items.headline: Adobe camera app’s new feature will critique your photos using AI
items.summary: Adobe's Project Indigo can now remove all kinds of backgrounds from photos you snap using the app.

items.summary: China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is

items.headline: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none

items.headline: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity,

items.headline: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
items.summary: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector data

items.headline: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
items.summary: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully

items.headline: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
items.summary: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: mo

items.summary: For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm. At its annual I/O developer c

items.headline: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising

items.summary: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red

items.summary: Halliday’s G2 glasses can listen to and summarize your workplace meetings—with no video involved.

items.summary: Last month, more than a hundred Stanford students left their own graduation to protest Google’s military contracts and deals with ICE. Two organizers, Amanda Campos and Eva Jones, tell us why.

items.headline: The Army Is Burning Through Its AI Tokens
items.summary: Members of the Army received an email informing them that they were rapidly depleting their AI tokens, and needed to limit use.

items.headline: Etsy Is In Its Flop Era, and Sellers Are Fleeing
items.summary: Once a quirky bastion of amateur vulva jewelry and pet portraits, Etsy is now deluged with mass-produced goods and AI knockoffs. Some customers don’t seem to mind.

items.summary: “Context bombing” tricks malicious AI agents into shutting down before they can do harm.

items.summary: The City Attorney’s Office sent the tech giants cease-and-desist letters this week telling them to stop profiting from 13 “face-swap” apps that are overwhelmingly used to target women and girls.
